title: Topcoder O/R F2F - Submissions Tab has Winners Marked in it but shouldn't
summary: This task involves creating an ETL blueprint to address the issue of winners being incorrectly displayed in the Submissions tab. The solution should include a detailed pipeline, validation queries, and data quality checks to ensure the integrity of the data.

artifacts.pipeline_spec: The ETL pipeline will extract data from the submissions database, transform it to exclude winners from the Submissions tab, and load the cleaned data back into the system. The pipeline will run daily to ensure data is up to date.
sql_snippets: UPDATE submissions SET display_status = 'hidden' WHERE winner_flag = TRUE;
artifacts.python_snippets:
def etl_process():
    log('Starting ETL process')
    data = extract_data()
    transformed_data = transform_data(data)
    load_data(transformed_data)
    log('ETL process completed')

data_quality_checks.name: Check for winners in submissions
data_quality_checks.threshold: Should be FALSE for all records in the Submissions tab.

data_quality_checks.name: Validate submission counts
data_quality_checks.threshold: Count of submissions should match expected numbers post-transformation.

assumptions: Access to the source data and schemas is not provided.
Specific transformation steps are not detailed in the prompt.
Acceptance criteria for the ETL process are not explicitly defined.
plan: Review the existing data structure for submissions and winners.
Define the source data schema and identify necessary transformations.
Create SQL snippets for data extraction and transformation.
Develop Python pseudo-code for the ETL process, including logging and checkpoints.
Draft data quality checks to validate the correctness of the ETL process.
Compile all components into a comprehensive ETL blueprint document.
validations: Self-check on the ETL process logic to ensure no winners are included in the Submissions tab.
SQL snippets tested against a sample dataset to validate correctness.

rubric_self_check.overall_notes: The ETL blueprint is partially complete but lacks specific details on source data and transformation steps.
